text: In academia you're always battling imposter syndrome. Everybody seems so smart. You go to conferences and people are giving these amazing talks and they're brilliant — and you're lost within 30 seconds. The thing is, everybody else feels the exact same way.
name: Daniel Lametti

text: I would say to enjoy it more. Don't fight so hard against everything.
name: Dawn Rouse
role: PhD in Integrated Studies in Education, 2012

text: My dissertation is good — it's not perfect, it's not amazing, but it's done.
name: Lorie Kloda

text: Make the PhD your own. It can open so many doors and can provide so many opportunities to learn and grow, and you have a lot of agency to take from it what you want.
name: Erin Parkes

text: The reward schedules are very sparse in research. It's just constant rejection and, all of a sudden, once in a while — maybe once a year, once every two years — you get a reward and that strings you along.
name: Daniel Lametti

text: Teaching is really important to me and it's what I am really good at, and that's okay. Not every academic has to be a researcher.
name: Dawn Rouse
role: PhD in Integrated Studies in Education, 2012

text: One of the most predictive factors for success in a PhD program is the relationship you have with your advisor.
name: Jessica Toste
role: PhD in Educational and Counselling Psychology, 2011

text: My self-esteem was pretty high as a librarian but my self-esteem as a PhD student was very low.
name: Lorie Kloda

text: There is a lot of gray area, a lot of jobs, a lot of different ways to set up a career — it's really not black or white.
name: Lance Rappaport

text: If you're looking for unconventional ways forward in your career, you have to work a little harder.
name: Dorothy Maguire

text: Living the PhD student experience has enhanced my empathy for people in different roles in higher education. Empathy serves me extremely well in the work I currently do — and in life outside work.
name: Carolyn Samuel
role: PhD in Integrated Studies in Education, 2016

text: I think there's this feeling in academia that once you leave, you can't come back, but I don't think that's necessarily true.
name: Daniel Lametti
